ignorePatterns:
**/*.min.*
**/*.generated.*
**/*.pyc
**/__pycache__/**
**/.pytest_cache/**
dist/**
coverage/**
node_modules/**
target/**
perf/results/**
docs/archive/**
**/*.snap
**/fixtures/**/expected/**
instructions: Treat AGENTS.md and .kiro/nginx-development-guide.md as primary review constraints. Prioritize regressions in streaming semantics, fail-open correctness, memory bounds, UTF-8 chunk boundaries, metrics schema consistency, and docs/tooling sync.

rules.rule: Do not introduce breaking changes to documented public behavior unless the PR explicitly describes compatibility impact, rollout implications, and migration guidance.

Behavior changes to negotiation, caching, streaming or buffering, fallback, parser, sanitizer, metrics, or tooling validation must include targeted regression tests (or a concrete rationale for why tests are not needed).
Changes affecting installation, configuration, runtime behavior, operator-visible semantics, or release/rollout guidance should update the relevant docs and validators in the same PR.
When C/Rust FFI interfaces, option defaults, or error mappings change, keep Rust code, C headers/callers, tests, and operator-facing docs synchronized in one change set.
For tooling validators/parsers, avoid regex constructs with overlapping quantifiers and prefer deterministic parsing for structured docs/tables.
